description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese Shinto purification fountain, known as a chozuya or temizuya, typically found at the entrance of shrines. It features a rectangular stone basin supported by two vertical wooden posts and covered by a gently sloped, blue-tiled roof with decorative ridge elements. A bamboo ladle and dipper rest on top of the basin, used for ritual hand and mouth washing. To one side, there is a small stone lantern, adding to the historical and cultural ambiance. The structure is set on a black platform, highlighting its ceremonial function. The overall design is simple yet elegant, with natural materials and subtle ornamentation reflecting traditional Japanese aesthetics.
description: A traditional Japanese Shinto purification fountain with a blue-tiled roof, stone basin, bamboo ladle, and a stone lantern, set on a black platform.
description_view_0: The left side view shows the sloped blue roof, the bamboo ladle on the stone basin, and the stone lantern in the background.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the entrance to the fountain, with the bamboo ladle and dipper clearly visible atop the basin and the stone lantern to the right.
description_view_2: The right side view displays the stone lantern more prominently, the wooden posts, and the bamboo ladle resting on the basin.
description_view_3: The rear view focuses on the back of the roof and basin, with the bamboo dipper and the structure's support elements visible.
